name: Southwestern Stuffed Peppers
recipeIngredient: 6 large Green, Red or Yellow Peppers
1/2 lb Italian sausage or chorizo (casing removed)
1 package (8 ounces) Pepperidge Farm Bread Crumbs
2 cups Shredded Monterey Jack Cheese with Jalapeno Peppers
1 can (14 1/2 ounces) Swanson Roasted Chicken Breast
recipeInstructions:
1. With a sharp knife, slice the tops off the 6 peppers; discard seeds and membranes.
2. In a 5-quart saucepan, place the peppers in enough boiling salted water to cover. Reduce heat to low. Cover; simmer for 5 minutes. Drain peppers on paper towels. Empty water from saucepan.
3. In a 10-inch skillet over medium heat, brown sausage with garlic, stirring to separate meat.
4. In a large bowl, combine sausage, stuffing, cheese and 3/4 cup of the broth. Toss to mix well. Fill peppers with stuffing mixture.
5. In a 5-quart saucepan, combine remaining broth and chopped red pepper. Arrange stuffed peppers in broth. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low; cover; simmer for 20 minutes or until peppers are tender and filling is hot. Remove stuffed peppers to serving platter; keep warm.
6. In a covered blender container, carefully blend red pepper-broth mixture until smooth. Serve with stuffed peppers.
Makes 6 servings.
